reasoning: The Lean statement faithfully formalizes the informal claim. `centralizer {x⁻¹ * a * x}` is the centralizer of the single element $x^{-1}ax$ (matching $C(x^{-1}ax)$), and `(fun g => x⁻¹ * g * x) '' (centralizer {a})` is precisely $\{x^{-1}gx : g \in C(a)\}$, which is the standard interpretation of $x^{-1}C(a)x$.
